risk_rationale: This allocator is the sole source of fileID uniqueness that engine/catalog CRUD (1.1.5), engine/mvcc, and engine/split will all depend on for correctness; a bug here (duplicate fileID under concurrency, or a high-water-mark that regresses on reopen) would silently corrupt catalog integrity system-wide rather than fail loudly. Concurrency-sensitive: must be verified with -race.

downstream_consumers.dependency: will call IDAllocator.Next() when creating a new CatalogRecord to populate CatalogRecord.FileID
assumes fileID uniqueness/no-reuse per docs/LLD/catalog.md and docs/LLD/mvcc.md
redirectTargetIDs reference fileIDs; must never collide with a reused ID

on_disk_format_impact.new_artifact: <catalog-path>.idalloc sidecar file (8 bytes, little-endian uint64 high-water-mark)
on_disk_format_impact.compat_note: Sidecar approach chosen specifically to avoid changing file.go's already-verified (task-1.1.3, PASS_WITH_COMMENTS) page-0 bitmap on-disk layout.

concurrency_impact.locking_added: sync.Mutex scoped entirely within IDAllocator (not global catalog lock)
concurrency_impact.note: Per task brief: a mutex here is a narrow allocator-internal lock, not a violation of catalog.md's 'no global lock' CRUD design principle (1.1.5 scope).

rollback_plan: Single isolated commit adding two new files only; revert is a clean single-commit revert with zero blast radius on other engine/catalog files.
